price_details: couldn't find any pricing on his site or in reviews, so this is inquire-only as far as i can tell

price_details: Price details are thin. This is a collective/squad model rather than one named photographer, and the only figure that surfaced was one reviewer's own booking: under $2k for two hours of coverage. No tiers or add-ons listed anywhere I could find.
notes: Went looking into this one because a few threads kept recommending photography squads as a budget move, and this is apparently a collective you get matched with rather than one specific person. A commenter said their photographer, Kate, was under $2k for two hours and called her awesome. Worth knowing going in that you may not get to hand pick who shoots your day, which is a real tradeoff for the price.

price_details: No pricing anywhere I checked. Their site didn't turn up in my search and I only have secondhand mentions to go on, so budget for a real quote before assuming anything.
notes: Notes from a reddit thread: - Came up in a list of editorial, luxe look recs alongside The Iris Photography and Rebecca Milan - Direct quote was just 'I went with...Jordan Gresham Photography', no detail on the actual experience - Nothing on price, coverage hours, or turnaround, so this is a name to research further, not a vetted pick

price_details: no pricing listed anywhere i found. he's positioned as a micro wedding specialist so probably scales down for smaller days, but you'd have to ask
notes: ok so i went down a reddit hole on micro wedding photographers and his name kept popping up (lots of love, one person said 'we used nick sparks for our micro wedding and he was fantastic'). the notes on him mention he helps with location recs and even got permits for couples, which is a nice touch if you're doing something outdoorsy tbh. no site or price info anywhere though so you'd have to reach out cold. seems like a solid pick for smaller/elopement-style days specifically

price_details: no site or pricing found, only a name drop in a reddit recommendation thread, so treat this as an unverified lead
notes: Review roundup: - Named alongside The Iris Photography, Jordan Gresham and Amanda Berube as picks for editorial, luxe looks - Direct quote was just a couple listing her among vendors they went with, no detail on price or delivery - No pricing page, no second source to confirm anything beyond the name, so start with an inquiry email before you get attached

price_details: Elopement sessions run about $3k for 2 hours including a travel fee for anything within 5 hours of Denver per a regional pricing note, and packages more broadly start at $4k. Full day wedding pricing isn't published, so ask directly if that's what you need.
notes: Went down a review rabbit hole on this one since she's specifically an elopement photographer and planner, not just a wedding shooter. Her site talks a lot about acting as a planning resource too, sending a custom elopement guide with location maps and packing lists on top of the actual photos, which is a nice value-add if you're doing something small and DIY. A reddit thread recommending affordable options for elopements had someone say flatly, "Emily May is your girl." No indication of what full wedding day coverage costs though, so that part is a blind spot.

price_details: Wedding coverage starts at $6,500 and videography (16mm and Super 8, plus digital) is a separate add-on from $7,250. He shoots 35mm and medium format film alongside digital. Site doesn't break out engagement session pricing or list what hours and deliverables come with the minimum package.
notes: Nine years in and he covers a wide territory, Aspen, Telluride, Vail, Denver, Boulder plus destination jobs in Iceland and New Orleans, but he's local to Boulder per a reddit commenter who used him after getting married in Iceland. Same comment recommended checking out Sam Marz too if he's booked. Site copy leans documentary forward and describes the work as a true to life yet poetic take on the couple's day, and one testimonial specifically praised his elopement photo and video coverage.

price_details: site had no pricing listed and I couldn't find a rate anywhere, so budget for an inquiry before you get attached to the look
notes: Their whole thing is documentary style, which tracks with the reddit mention I found where someone recommending photographers for that specific look said simply, 'for documentary style, hennessy photo co.' that's basically the entirety of the firsthand info out there right now, no review detail on responsiveness or turnaround, so this reads more like a name to shortlist than a vetted pick

price_details: no pricing published on their site, though they market themselves as an affordable option, so it's worth an inquiry if budget is the priority
notes: Site describes them as a creative team, plural photographers rather than a solo shooter, serving Boulder, Denver, Fort Collins, Pine and Estes Park, so a pretty wide service area. A vendor account on reddit name dropped them alongside BlueSpruce Photography as an established Denver option when someone asked for affordable recs, though nobody in that thread gave a specific number.
service_region: Boulder, Denver, Fort Collins, Pine, Estes Park area

price_details: Wedding collections begin at $8,500, with select date associate photography (a second shooter or team option rather than Samuel himself) starting at $6,500. Minimum of 8 hours coverage, engagement sessions and a second photographer are both available add-ons. No call required to book according to a regional note, which is a nice change from vendors who gatekeep pricing behind a consultation.
notes: Based up in Fort Collins but comes up constantly in threads as someone who'll travel for the right wedding. Multiple reddit mentions call him documentary minded and describe him as willing to travel for Denver area weddings too. His own site pricing is more detailed than most in this batch, naming an actual 8 hour minimum and specific add-ons instead of just a starting number, which made it easier to compare against other quotes.

price_details: her published pricing page is for NYC lifestyle sessions specifically (full day unlimited packages, additional hours from $300 to $350, a travel fee scaled to distance outside the NYC subway network, a rush edit option for $300, RAW files from $100), so none of those numbers should be assumed to carry over to a Colorado booking
notes: Her site is clearly built around an NYC client base first, packages are named things like 'NYC Lifestyle Sessions' and travel fees are benchmarked off the NYC subway network, but a Colorado wedding thread still called her out by name as 'budget and travel friendly,' so she's apparently working destination jobs out here too. Worth confirming what a CO specific quote looks like since none of the posted tiers are written with Colorado in mind. Nice touch on her site: rush editing is available for an extra fee if you need photos back in 2 to 3 days instead of the standard 7 to 14.

price_details: no site or pricing found anywhere, only a passing reddit mention alongside Love & Lens, so treat this purely as a lead to research
notes: - Vendor flair account on reddit named them alongside Love & Lens as 'established Denver photographers with great,' comment cut off before finishing the thought - nothing else to go on here, no price, no review detail, worth an inquiry email but not much else to judge by yet

price_details: no site or pricing turned up anywhere, so this is inquire only for now

A separate commenter in the same thread described her style as having an airy but real life color vibe and said she was shooting their engagement photos ahead of a June wedding. Take the first review with a grain of salt given the disclosed bias, but the style description lines up across both.

price_details: no pricing found on a site or anywhere else i checked so you'd need to inquire directly
notes: came up in the same reddit thread as hennessy photo co and alyssa carpenter when someone asked for colorado wedding photographer recs. the quote was pretty enthusiastic: 'emma rose photography is beautifullllll, she did our engagement session and several people have told me they are the most beautiful wedding related photos they've ever seen,' with a pic-time gallery link attached to back it up. that's engagement photos specifically being praised though, no firsthand account of full wedding day coverage that i could find

price_details: No pricing anywhere, and no site either as far as I could tell, just an Instagram presence, so this one is inquire only and lightly vetted.

price_details: No pricing anywhere online, she doesn't even have a website up yet, just an instagram.
notes: Found her through a reddit thread on Colorado elopement photographers where someone just said 'check out Spenser, she's amazing' and linked her instagram, no other details on cost or what a session looks like. Following the account for now since there's not much else to go on.

price_details: No site to check pricing on, going off a Denver subreddit thread instead.
notes: A regional pricing note describes her style as ethereal with photos shot far away and wide, which lines up with a reddit poster who was seriously considering booking her but flagged the same thing as a concern, said all her sample photos read as very far away and she wanted shots that felt closer up. No word on packages or cost since there's no working site to check.

price_details: Site has no package pricing listed, just client testimonials about delivered albums, so cost is an inquiry-only conversation with Barb.
notes: I went looking for numbers and came up empty, but the reviews for this Boulder photographer are consistently glowing. One couple said Barb captured the spirit of their evening and that guests kept commenting on her energy and humor. Another review specifically praised how she guided guests into natural shots without ever feeling intrusive. A testimonial on the site mentions an album so good it made coworkers tear up at the office.

price_details: Estes Park based. Tiered by hours: 4hrs=$2,500, 6hrs=$3,000, 8hrs=$3,500, 10hrs=$4,250, unlimited=$5,000. Second photographer $550/day. Albums start at $700. Travel to Front Range $150, Vail/Breckenridge $300, 5+ hrs away $500 plus hotel. A la carte rate also listed: $750 first hour, $650 each additional.
notes: Price breakdown: Estes Park photographer Rannveig runs a straightforward hourly menu, 4 hours for $2,500 up to unlimited coverage for $5,000. - Second photographer is a flat $550/day add-on, albums run $700+ - Travel is tiered too: $150 to the Front Range, $300 to Vail or Breck, $500+hotel if you're 5+ hours out - Reviews span a decade (2015-2021) and all land on the same note: she's efficient at organizing bridal parties and gets natural, unposed shots, one reviewer said she stayed later than expected to catch every night shot they wanted

price_details: no dollar figures posted anywhere on her site, just what's included per package tier, so you gotta reach out for actual numbers
notes: ok so this Durango photographer splits into two tiers, elopements/intimate weddings get 4 hours min w/ location scouting + digital files + online gallery, full weddings are 8 hours min and that tier adds a second shooter. no pricing on either tier tho which is annoying. reviews are stacked though, multiple people say she made them feel comfortable in front of the camera (one bride literally said Abbi helped carry her dress when no one else was around lol) and the engagement + wedding combo reviews keep coming up as a package people book together

price_details: Site just says 'for curated pricing, inquire now,' no packages or numbers listed publicly. With 115 reviews she's clearly established, so expect a real consultation process rather than prices up front.
notes: Telluride based photographer with a huge volume of five star reviews, which is rare to see this consistent. Multiple reviewers use almost the same language, calling her calm and a step ahead of the day rather than reactive to it, and more than one mentioned she felt both everywhere and invisible at once. A January 2026 review said she supported the couple through the whole wedding weekend, not just the ceremony day itself. No pricing means you're booking on reputation alone here, which given the review count feels like less of a gamble than usual.

price_details: Important heads up: their own site says they no longer cover full size weddings, elopements only now. No pricing listed for that either.
notes: Big one to flag here, Above Alpine's site states outright that they stopped covering full weddings and only offer elopement services these days, so this only works if you're planning something small. The reviews on file predate that shift and describe Andy shooting full events with video and music add-ons, calling his eye creative and his turnaround quick, but that's not what you'd be booking now. Worth a direct message to confirm what an elopement package actually includes before getting attached.

price_details: Wedding packages start at $3,000, site says clients typically invest $3,000-$4,000. Includes engagement session, styling guide, 1-2 photographers, all edited images, online gallery with print release. Engagement-only sessions start at $550. Custom payment plans offered on every package.
notes: Based in Severance and her pricing is actually posted, which is refreshing: wedding packages start at $3,000 with most couples landing in the $3,000-$4,000 range, and that includes an engagement session plus 1-2 photographers plus a print release, not just digital files. Standalone engagement sessions run from $550. She also offers custom payment plans on everything, which the site mentions more than once. With 98 reviews at a full 5 stars, one describing her as a last-minute save after the couple's original photographer got sick, this seems like a safe, well-documented pick if the natural-light look works for you.

price_details: Site is all elopement package marketing copy with zero dollar amounts, you have to inquire for a quote.
notes: Adam runs this as a dedicated Crested Butte and Breckenridge elopement photographer, not a general wedding shooter, and the reviews back that up hard, all describing adventurous mountain elopements rather than traditional ceremonies. One PA couple said he handled the full timeline for their Breckenridge elopement and made the whole out-of-state planning process painless. Another review mentions he coordinated logistics for a group that traveled in from Ohio. No pricing posted anywhere though, so budget for a real conversation before you get attached to the look.

price_details: No pricing anywhere on their site, photo and video packages aren't broken out publicly either.
notes: Hans and Desiree run this Durango studio and shoot a lot of elopements out toward Silverton and the San Juan Mountains. A review describes them rerouting an entire elopement around a snowstorm without losing communication, and another mentions Hans hopping on an ATV to chase a better vantage point up the pass. Reviewers consistently call out flexibility and fast learning of guest names on group shots, one saying he wrangled 13 people into position within minutes. No pricing means this is an inquire-first booking.

price_details: Site only offers a pricing guide on request, no packages or numbers published, photo and video are sold together as collections.
notes: Ben Eng shoots out of Telluride and specializes in adventure elopements around Ouray and the surrounding San Juans, offering photo and video as combined collections rather than separate hires. Reviews describe him as unobtrusive on the wedding day itself, one couple at Gorrono Ranch saying guests barely noticed he was there despite him getting every shot. Multiple reviewers mention his knowledge of the terrain leading to better light and better locations than they expected. You have to request the pricing guide directly since nothing is posted.

price_details: Elopement and intimate wedding collections start at $1,800 per the site FAQ, full wedding packages aren't priced publicly (PDF rate cards referenced but not accessible). Second photographer offered as an add-on fee. Travel fee applies outside Durango, amount not stated. All packages include a luxury wedding album.
notes: Durango-based, and elopement/intimate collections start at $1,800 with a real album included standard, though full wedding pricing isn't posted and you'd need to request the rate card directly. Second shooter is an optional add-on and there's a travel fee once you're outside Durango, though the site doesn't say how much. A Florida couple planning a Colorado destination wedding specifically praised how thorough she was on an initial phone call walking through the whole process. With 54 reviews at 4.9 stars, more than one mentions choosing her specifically for adventurous mountain-view elopement shots over pricier local competitors.

price_details: No packages or numbers on the site, just an 'Investment' page header with no figures under it.
notes: Benjamin works out of Boulder and shoots both weddings and other event photography, not just weddings exclusively. One review from a Boettcher Mansion wedding said guests reacted to the photos with genuine surprise at how good they turned out, and another from an organization's annual convention praised his attention to detail outside the wedding context entirely. Reviewers across both types of work call the whole process relaxed and stress-free rather than rushed. No pricing means you're inquiring cold on cost.

price_details: site lists an Essential Collection (up to 4hrs) and a step-up tier (up to 6hrs) plus a $500 add for a second shooter, but no dollar figures anywhere for the packages themselves so you have to inquire for the actual rates
notes: -been shooting durango weddings for 10+ yrs, every package comes w/ a complimentary engagement session -luxury album included on the bigger tier, 2nd shooter is a $500 add-on -google reviews (96 of them, all 5) keep mentioning she took couples out to lake vallecitos for the mountain shots -also does elopements, site leans hard into "classic, candid, romantic" as her words for style

venue: Alton Richardson Studios | Boulder Wedding Photographer
price_text: Local coverage starts at $2,200, elopements from $900
price_details: Front Range coverage starts at $2,200 per the site's own FAQ, elopement/micro wedding coverage starts at $900. A 30 minute video call plus a signed contract and 33% non-refundable retainer locks the date. Engagement and couples sessions are offered as add-ons, no dollar figure published for those.
notes: Read through a stack of his reviews and a pattern showed up fast: multiple couples specifically called themselves not photogenic and said Alton coached them through it so the awkwardness didn't show. One review mentioned he handled picking the shoot locations entirely, which took a task off their plate. He shot both engagement and wedding for at least two of the reviewers. Available for CO, WY, and NM plus travel elsewhere per his site.

price_details: No prices published, she asks you to fill out a contact form for her pricing guide then meet by phone or Zoom before sending an invoice. Heirloom album is an add-on you can tack on anytime.
notes: - Specialty: elopements & micro weddings across Western Colorado, the San Juan Mountains, and Moab, Utah - Started the business in 2012, moved to Grand Junction in 2015 - One review said she helped navigate national park permits and kept guests organized for group shots - Multiple reviewers called out how responsive she was leading up to the ceremony, one used the word "communicative" twice

price_details: full custom proposal only, inquire for pricing including travel. Site says the weddings she shoots tend to be destination, multi day events so packages get built per couple rather than off a set menu.
notes: andrea does full day mountain wedding coverage across colorado (getting ready through reception) and also runs a side company called bound for the mountains for smaller intimate weddings and elopements lol. one review said their original plans got altered last minute and she rolled with it. another mentioned she partnered well with their outside wedding planner on the day of logistics. reviews go back to 2020 and stay 5 star the whole way through

price_details: -2 photographers included on every collection, no hidden fees per the site -heirloom album also included, sneak peek gallery in 72hrs, full gallery in 8-10 weeks -20% retainer locks the date, remaining balance due 30 days out, payment plans available -launchintel digest backs this up: "starts at $4,500"
notes: -christine (owner) got called out by name in 3+ reviews for being easy to reach and calm on the day -one review said they came from out of state and did a small elopement, said having someone super responsive made that distance easier -another mentioned videography is also offered as part of the team's work -moody/editorial editing style comes up in a couple reviews as the draw

price_details: Their pricing page exists but the copy we could pull just had the page title, no actual numbers, so you'd need to reach out directly for rates.
notes: Went digging on this one and the reviews we found skew heavily toward family sessions, newborn shoots, and gender reveals rather than weddings specifically. Ashley (the photographer) gets consistent praise for making kids comfortable in front of the camera and for balancing candid vs posed shots. Worth confirming directly whether wedding day coverage is something she still actively books, since none of the top reviews mention a wedding.

price_details: Wedding packages average $4,100 with 100-150 images delivered per hour of coverage and a complimentary engagement session included. Microwedding/elopement packages average $2,500, with a smaller stripped down option around $600. He's Denver-based but shoots statewide and beyond; for out of state travel his fees only cover plane ticket/gas plus room and board per the site, no extra markup mentioned.
notes: couples in his reviews keep bringing up how he met with them beforehand and did their engagement shoot first, so wedding day felt like working with someone they already knew. one reviewer said they were awkward in front of a camera (their word, lol) but got guided into it naturally. delivery is all digital now, online gallery stays live a year with a print/album store built in. no physical usb anymore per the site.

price_details: not itemized in what we saw)
notes: so this is a photo AND video team, not just stills, they do both under one booking. 40% retainer up front, rest due 4 weeks before the wedding. no actual dollar figures came through on the pricing page for us though, just structure/policy stuff, so you'd have to go through their site or a call to get real numbers. heads up though, elopement and ceremony-only packages aren't available for saturday or holiday weekend dates per their site.
photos: one review mentioned meeting the team (cylina, gretchen the photographer, rocco the videographer) at a wedding expo first and getting sold after the team pulled up a video from the couple's actual venue as a work sample lol, that's a nice touch tbh. another reviewer said they won their package in a giveaway and insisted the quality didn't feel different from a paid booking. covers colorado plus parts of wyoming and nebraska, and they'll build a custom package for destination weddings too.

price_details: Site pricing copy we saw was mostly policy language (elopement/simple ceremony framing, a $1.25/mi travel fee past 30mi from Inverness, CO), no actual package dollar amounts came through, so rates are on inquiry.
notes: Andrew (the photographer) shows up by name in a big chunk of the 114 reviews here and multiple couples specifically called the pricing affordable relative to what they got. A few reviews mention it being a more intimate, one-on-one style shoot and said he made nervous first timers feel at ease quickly. Custom packages are apparently easy to arrange, one review said he was flexible with add-ons.

price_text: Elopements start at $2,000, weddings start at $4,000
price_details: Weddings start at $4,000 and elopements start at $2,000. A 50% deposit holds the date, remaining balance due 30 days before, and payment plans (monthly or quarterly auto withdrawal) are offered if the lump sum doesn't work. A second photographer is always brought along per the site, named as Madison or Annah.
notes: -eleanor is the photographer behind this one and she comes up by name constantly in reviews, does weddings but also a lot of family/senior/headshot work per the reviews -one review specifically mentioned an out of state couple (FL & TX) who booked a 30 min engagement shoot and had rain in the forecast, said eleanor kept them updated the whole time -launchintel digest calls the style "emotive storytelling and heartfelt portraits" -129 reviews, all 5 star, feels like a volume shop but the couples reviews read genuinely happy

price_details: Couldn't find a pricing page on their site at all, nothing came through for us to check, so this looks like an inquire-only situation.
notes: - Photographer: Kay. Reviews go back to 2009 for at least one family who hired her as their wedding photographer originally - Same family has kept hiring her for family photos for over a decade since, including twins from babies onward per one review - Reviews lean heavily toward family/maternity/newborn/senior work rather than weddings specifically, worth confirming she still books weddings before reaching out

price_details: Site doesn't list set packages, just says custom packages are built after you request a quote and get on a call. A launchintel digest pegs it starting around $2,300 per The Knot, for photo plus video.
notes: -beau is the photographer/owner, comes up by name in basically every review -multiple reviews mention he was their preferred photographer specifically at ken caryl vista, so he's got venue familiarity there -2nd photographers and videographers can be added per the site -reviewers keep saying turnaround was quick and he's fast to respond to emails

price_details: Site mentions "Weddings & Elopements" as a service line but all the actual package/pricing copy we found was for headshot and branding sessions (30 min sessions, 5-10 retouched images typical), nothing wedding-specific came through, so wedding rates aren't published anywhere we could check.
notes: bob's reviews are almost entirely headshots/branding/team photos and family portraits rather than weddings specifically. one review does call him professional and easygoing on a work photoshoot and says he delivers images that feel natural rather than stiff and posed. worth confirming directly that wedding day coverage is still something he actively books given how little wedding-specific content shows up.

price_details: Site lists a starting rate of $3800 for wedding packages, every tier includes a complimentary engagement session and complimentary album design, per WPJA her associate Mike Kory books separately starting around $2800
notes: I looked into Bonnie Photo out of Erie for a while and kept coming back to it. Packages start at $3800 and every single one comes with a free engagement session plus album design built in, which is a nice chunk of stuff not itemized separately. She and her partner Mike shoot candid, bold, vibrant work and are explicitly LGBTQ+ and BIPOC inclusive, and one review mentioned the engagement session actually helped the couple loosen up for the wedding photos since neither of them liked being photographed. She's based in Boulder county but seems to work mountain settings too.

price_details: Site says elopement collections start at $1800 for coverage of 10 people or less, pricing is by coverage type rather than by the hour, no full wedding pricing published so ask about that separately
notes: This is a team of Breckenridge based photographers who specialize hard in elopements, their site says coverage is for 10 people or max and pricing is by what's included rather than time on site. Packages start at $1800 for that scale. One review described getting paired with a photographer for a surprise dog sledding proposal that turned into a whole planned experience, and another said they adapted fast when a winter shoot got rained out by weather. All the reviews I found were glowing, no complaints showed up in my search.

price_details: Site lists packages starting at 895 for midweek bookings, a 50% deposit plus signed contract locks the date, engagement or proposal sessions apply toward a full wedding package with their sister company anywhere in the world
notes: Found this one while looking at Breckenridge options and their site has actual numbers up front, which was refreshing. Packages start at $895 for midweek dates, and if you do an engagement or proposal session first that cost rolls into a full wedding package later even if the wedding isn't in Colorado. A couple of reviews specifically praised Sarah for knowing the local terrain, she shot one ski elopement and one ceremony up on Boreas Pass and both reviewers called out how well she handled the outdoor light and logistics. Deposit is 50% to hold your date.

price_details: Couldn't get their site to load, it's a Facebook page rather than a real site and no pricing shows up there, would have to message them directly for wedding rates
notes: I tried to dig into Breckenridge Photographics and hit a wall fast, their web presence is basically just a Facebook bookmark link and it wouldn't load for me at all. The reviews that do exist are all about the physical camera shop in town and a guy named David helping people pick out film cameras and gear, not about wedding coverage. Nothing I found actually confirms they shoot weddings, so treat this as a maybe and call before you get your hopes up.
sources: site failed to load; google reviews

price_details: Site just has a single tagline about their photography collection, no package tiers or numbers listed, you'd need to reach out directly for wedding pricing
notes: Breckenridge Portrait Company's site is pretty thin, just a line about their photography collection and nothing on actual pricing. The one review I found that felt relevant wasn't even about a wedding, it was a Christmas Eve family shoot where the family got stuck in the snow and showed up late, and the photographer Scott was patient about it and got the shots done anyway including with a toddler who needed a nap. Rating is a mixed 3.7 stars off just 3 reviews so there isn't much to go on either way.

price_text: Quote only for full weddings; elopement packages advertised separately
price_details: Site is built around Colorado elopement packages with pricing mentioned but no dollar figures actually published on the pages I checked, full wedding pricing isn't listed at all so you'd inquire directly
notes: Brenna Nicole Photography out of Lone Tree has basically built her whole brand around Colorado elopements, her site talks a lot about guiding couples through the when/where/how of an elopement day but I couldn't find actual numbers published anywhere. Reviews consistently describe her as warm and easy to talk to, one bride said an intro call alone sold them before they'd even met in person. Another review mentioned she helped coordinate the whole day, not just showing up to shoot, which seems to be a recurring theme with her.

price_details: their site doesn't list any package prices, it's mostly elopement planning copy and blog content about dress shopping, so you have to inquire for rates directly
notes: -mal & roo run this as a two person elopement photo + video + planning team based in colorado,-a wedding planner reviewer specifically praised roo for pulling together a late-april elopement on a short timeframe,-another review calls roo 'a creative genius' who suggested ideas during a brainstorming call before the shoot,-site leans hard into nature/adventure elopements not full weddings so ask if you want a big-guestlist day covered

price_details: Classic Coverage is $6,100 with a second photographer for 5 hours and a 60 min complimentary engagement session, Weekend Coverage is $8,500 with 12 hours of second-shooter time and a 90 min engagement session, elopements start at $1,250 for weekday coverage within 30 min of Denver (90 min, 5-10 guests, Saturdays and most Fri/Sun blocked May-Oct under 6 hrs), rehearsal dinner coverage is $1,000, boudoir add-on $800, mountain engagement travel is $300, albums start at $700 (family albums $500, $100 off if bought with the package), nonrefundable retainer required to book
notes: Went looking into Catherine because she shoots a lot of Denver and Fort Collins weddings and delivers 75-100 edited images per hour of coverage, which is a nice concrete number to plan a gallery around. A wedding musician who worked a reception alongside her said she has a calm, professional energy and floats around without being intrusive during key moments, which is basically the review you want from a fellow vendor. Another couple said their engagement shoot in September felt seamless start to finish and that she made them comfortable in front of the camera, not a given for two people who described themselves as not super photogenic. All three reviews I found were five stars, so no warts surfaced yet, just consistently glowing.

price_details: couldn't find dollar figures on the pricing page, just the structure: half day (4 hrs) vs full day elopement packages, no travel fees anywhere in Colorado (out of state he only passes along actual travel cost), and every elopement booking includes a minimum of 3 rolls of film shot alongside digital, so you'd still need to call or email for actual numbers
notes: - Focus: colorado elopements and adventure days, not really a traditional big-wedding photographer from what the site shows,- Film shooter: every elopement gets at least 3 rolls of color or black and white film mixed in with digital, a nice touch if you want that look,- Reviews: multiple couples call him easy to work with and 'like a close friend,' one mentions he shot through a rainy Salida wedding that ended in a rainbow,- One reviewer said friends immediately booked him for their own wedding after seeing the results, always a good sign

price_details: the pricing page copy didn't have actual numbers listed anywhere we could see, just package descriptions, so you'd need to reach out directly for rates and video add-on costs
notes: Colt and Hannah work out of Durango and specialize in San Juan Mountains elopements, they mention Telluride and Silverton by name and say they'll travel to the Four Corners area including Moab, Sedona and Santa Fe. They shoot both photo and video, plus they've started weaving 35mm film into digital elopement shoots if you want that texture. notes: Based in Durango but her reviews mention shooting a wedding out in New Jersey too, so she'll travel for the right booking. Reviews lean heavily toward family/portrait work (newborns, maternity, headshots) alongside weddings, and one reviewer specifically praised how she scoped out photo spots early at an unfamiliar venue before the ceremony started. You do have to go through the phone call step before getting real numbers, which could be a dealbreaker if you want to price-shop quickly.

price_details: Site doesn't post rates on the packages/investments page, just describes coverage windows: 4+ hours recommended for elopements, 6-8 hours for weddings.
notes: - Based in Crested Butte but says available everywhere, now booking 2026 and 2027 - Also does filmmaking, not just stills - Site leans hard into elopement planning specifically, offers to help scout locations and plan the actual day, not just show up and shoot - A 2026 review said she was somehow everywhere at once without feeling intrusive, and asked thoughtful prep questions about vision and timeline beforehand - Multiple reviews mention a Kara working alongside her, so bookings might come with a second shooter by default, worth confirming

price_details: couldn't get their pricing page to load so no rates to report, sourced entirely from her google reviews instead.
notes: she's based in the eagle river valley near vail and does engagement + wedding + family portrait work, one reviewer said this was their 4th project with her over the years. what stood out from the reviews: when a couple mentioned they were still deciding between multiple photographers, she reportedly didn't badmouth the competitor at all, just explained the style difference, which is a nice sign. another reviewer said she helped with posing and kept people laughing to ease nerves. no pricing info to go on though so you'd have to call.

price_details: couldn't get their pricing page to load so nothing on rates to share here.
notes: Denver based, does both weddings and elopements per her reviews (one couple planned an elopement with her from a different time zone entirely, which she apparently managed fine). Instagram is @leahgoetzel. The recurring word across three separate reviews is 'calming' or 'positive energy,' one bride said she was the first and only photographer they even spoke to because the first meeting sold them. No pricing info at all though, so budget-conscious couples would need to call before getting excited.

price_details: No numbers published, you email or fill out a form to get a custom proposal. What she DOES publish: a signed contract plus a 40% retainer locks your date, and packages include a second photographer/assistant option plus high-res print-ready files.
notes: - Telluride based, shoots at recognizable local spots by name: San Sophia Overlook, Gorrono Ranch, The Peaks Resort, Palmyra Lookout - Style is 'light and airy,' handles both intimate elopements and multi-day destination wedding weekends - Reviewer flew in from Atlanta for a Telluride destination wedding and called her one of the best decisions they made - Another reviewer specifically praised fast response times and said she stayed calm and accommodating even with last-minute schedule changes

price_details: Basic wedding day coverage starts at $2,200. Most couples land between $3,500 and $5,000 for their full collection. Comprehensive packages start at $2,750 with customized options for smaller weddings. Mini Wedding packages include up to 6 hours, 2 photographers, an online gallery, download/print rights, and a credit toward a signature album; the full package bumps that to up to 8 hours. Portrait sessions run from $550, event coverage is $275/hour, engagement sessions can be added at a discount.

price_text: Quote only, custom package pricing via consultation
price_details: Site doesn't post flat rates, instead walks through the booking process: get in touch, get a contract plus retainer request, sign and pay retainer to lock your date/time. Some concrete perks though: $50 off for returning clients, $100 in print credit if you refer someone who books a wedding or elopement, and a $50 discount if you both get to the session by bike or walking (an actual green discount). Travel within 30 miles of Denver is included in every package; further out is case by case.
notes: She shoots weddings, elopements, engagements, couples, boudoir, and lifestyle sessions. Turnaround varies a lot by shoot type: sneak peeks in 3 days, but full weddings and elopements take 4 to 6 weeks for the gallery and another 3 to 6 weeks if you want prints or albums. A wedding planner who's worked with her repeatedly called her reliably kind, timely, and professional, and specifically praised how well she communicates with other vendors on-site, which planners don't always say about photographers.

price_details: custom quote, no published tiers, but two site lines both stress travel is fully included so that part is at least clear
notes: she does 1 hour of coverage per location with 2 outfit changes allowed per hour, and everything gets built into a custom proposal after a chat instead of picking from a menu. 4.9 stars across 86 google reviews and the recent ones keep saying the same thing, incredibly communicative and calm on the day. one review mentioned the album made it hard to pick favorites because so many came out well. documentary/candid style is the pitch, not posed.

price_details: no pricing on site - they lean into the relationship-first pitch (engagement session onward) instead of listing packages, so you'd need to inquire
notes: tao & maria, husband/wife team out of crested butte -call it an 'adventure session' instead of engagement session -only 6 google reviews but all 5 stars, one from 2019 says they took the couple out for tacos before the wedding to build comfort in front of the camera -another review specifically praised how natural/unstaged the shots looked, not the typical posed stuff -does elopements, one review mentions a waterfall ceremony during wildflower season

price_details: Site doesn't publish package prices. All collections include Mallory as lead with an option to add a second photographer, rehearsal coverage, and a custom album, and engagement sessions run 1.5 hours, so you'd inquire for actual numbers.
notes: Went looking into her reviews since the site is all vibes and no pricing. She splits her year living half in Bali and half in Denver and shoots weddings and elopements around the world as a result. A venue owner review said they hired her for their own wedding and called her genuinely something special among photographers they'd worked with professionally. Another bride mentioned payment installments being available which helped with planning cash flow.

price_details: Site lists a starting range but doesn't break down what's included at that tier or what pushes the price up - no mention of hours, add ons, or second shooter on the pages we could see.
notes: Review roundup: 20 years shooting weddings, 110+ five star google reviews, site claims 11 awards. Serves Fort Collins, Estes Park, Denver, Boulder, Breckenridge and Longmont per his own listing. One reviewer said 'book with Mark, you won't regret it' and called him easy to work with regardless of scenery. Another review called out fast, outstanding communication through the whole planning process
service_region: Fort Collins, Estes Park, Denver, Boulder, Breckenridge, Longmont

price_text: all-inclusive elopement packages, add-ons priced separately
price_details: this one's an elopement/micro-wedding package company not a solo photographer, so pricing bundles photography with the ceremony itself. no travel fee within 30 min of estes park, fees apply beyond that. marriage license is a separate $30 the couple gets themselves, extra guests run $20 each, and there's a 'save $300' promo mentioned on the amore package on their site
notes: okay so this is more of a full elopement package deal than just a photographer, kevin runs it and kennedy shoots photos, found a bunch of reviews mentioning both by name which is a good sign tbh. one review had a literal landslide delay their rmnp ceremony and kevin handled the reroute without drama. another said 40+mph winds forced a last minute move from outdoor to indoor and the team pulled it off anyway. kennedy specifically got praised for making picture taking easy even when people are nervous. rating's only 4.1 across 81 reviews though so not everyone loved it, worth digging into the lower ones before booking imo
service_region: Estes Park + Rocky Mountain National Park

price_details: couldn't get much off their site beyond a single blog style post about an elopement shoot on montezuma peak. no pricing or package info visible and no reviews to reference either.
notes: All I've got here is a blog post on the site about a styled elopement shoot up on Montezuma Peak, nothing on pricing or packages that we could find, and no reviews turned up to give a sense of what working with them is actually like. Based in Vail so presumably shoots mountain weddings and elopements in that area but that's an inference from location, not something the site spells out. Would need to reach out directly for basically everything.

price_details: Site has a full packages and pricing section by name but the actual numbers weren't visible to us, just page titles like elopement experiences and international adventure elopements.
notes: Twenty years doing wedding and elopement work based out of Telluride but he clearly travels a lot for it. site mentions shoots in the Pacific Northwest, Tofino, Yosemite and Dunton Hot Springs alongside the local Telluride stuff. A review from this spring said they interviewed several photographers before picking him specifically because he seemed like he'd flow with however the day went rather than force a shot list. Another couple had him for both their Telluride engagement and their Granby Ranch wedding a year apart and said the mountain setting came through alongside the emotional stuff. A third review mentioned he handled hail, rain and shine in the same day for a Crested Butte micro wedding without missing the wildflowers blooming that week.

price_details: He asks for a call to give accurate detailed pricing rather than posting rates, and mentions bundling in his wife's wedding officiant services at a discount if you want both from one vendor.
notes: Based in Eagle and covers Vail and Beaver Creek elopements from what the site says. Free unlimited consultations before booking to talk through photography preferences and day-of schedule. One review that stood out was a bride from North Carolina who'd never planned a proposal shoot before. he drove out to Beaver Creek to scout locations himself and FaceTimed her through the area beforehand so the surprise proposal would go smoothly. 4.9 stars across 71 reviews and multiple people specifically called out how easy and personable he was to work with.

price_text: $350-$1,100 depending on session length and package
price_details: She's on location only, no rental houses (except she'll make an exception for elopements). 30 min sessions run $350 for 10 images up to $740 for the full gallery, 1 hour sessions run $550 for 20 images up to $1,100 for the full gallery plus a 20 page layflat album. 25% deposit to reserve, non refundable per the site. Weekend sessions add a flat $50 surcharge, Friday doesn't.
notes: Pricing is actually posted here which is rare enough to be worth noting on its own. She's based in Breckenridge and does on location shoots only aside from elopements which get an exception to shoot at rental houses. A review mentioned booking her for a big 11 person extended family session and called the prices very reasonable especially for the area. Another reviewer highlighted her finding a mountain lake for senior photos and getting a large number of usable shots efficiently. 5 stars across 45 reviews with nothing negative that we saw.

price_details: 6 hours of coverage gets you 400+ edited images, 7 hours bumps to 500+, 8 hours to 600+. Average investment range quoted on site is $3,999-$6,000. Elopements start separately at $2,200. Add-ons include a second shooter, plus a 50% discounted engagement session OR 50% discounted bridal boudoir, one or the other not both. 25% retainer to book, rest due the day before the wedding.
notes: Based in Colorado Springs and does weddings, elopements and family sessions. She's upfront on her site that she's a wife and mom shooting Colorado weddings, and one review called out that she was detailed and direct throughout planning which made the day feel less stressful. Another review specifically said the pricing was very fair for what's included. 5 stars across 34 reviews and multiple mention her communication being clear from the first inquiry.

price_details: Site is built entirely around elopement packages (mini, half day, full day, multi day) but doesn't post actual dollar figures for any tier that we could find. $2,000 retainer secures your date once you sign the contract.
notes: She's elopement focused specifically, not full weddings, and includes a 60+ page elopement planning guide with the multi day package plus help finding locations based on how much hiking or off roading you're up for. Style leans documentary. One review said she guided a private vow exchange separate from a bigger wedding and shot both in one day. Another mentioned she went the extra mile scouting a shoot that involved wading into chest deep water for the shot. 5 stars across 96 reviews and several call out how organized and responsive she was from the first call.

price_details: Packages aren't priced on the pages we saw, but travel terms are spelled out clearly: 1 hour of travel from Fort Collins is included, beyond that it's a prorated $100/hour. 50% deposit due upfront to book, non-refundable, balance due after the shoot and before photos are delivered.
notes: Covers Fort Collins plus Estes Park, Loveland, Greeley, Longmont, Louisville, Lafayette and Boulder within that included travel radius. Their higher tier collection includes a second photographer specifically so getting-ready shots in two rooms at once don't come down to picking one. Reviews are shorter than most photographers we looked at but consistently glowing, one couple said their elopement photos turned out beautiful and Moon gave good local recommendations along the way. 4.7 stars but only 12 reviews so a smaller sample than others.
service_region: Fort Collins, Estes Park, Loveland, Greeley, Longmont, Boulder, Front Range

price_details: Site talks about crafting a personalized package after checking in with the couple and wedding team, no dollar figures listed. Album add-ons plus pre-wedding and engagement sessions are offered on top of the base package.
notes: Husband and wife team based in Vail, 4.9 stars across 40 reviews going back years. One review described an 18 degree New Year's Eve wedding where Jenny kept everyone's spirits up shooting outside in the cold and still got every shot on the list plus more. Another bride called the whole process from first call to final gallery delivery professional and organized, and said Jenny was everywhere on the day without ever feeling intrusive.

price_details: Booking process is spelled out: pick a package and payment plan, then sign a contract and pay a retainer to lock the date. No actual package prices listed on the pages we found.
notes: Michele and Mark are the team, based out of De Beque but their portfolio includes a Buena Vista elopement so they clearly range around the state. Style leans toward capturing joy and emotion over posed shots per multiple reviews. One review said they felt awkward in front of a camera going in and Michele put them at ease quickly. Another specifically praised how responsive and organized Michele was throughout planning, with Mark joining for the wedding day itself alongside her. 5 stars across 77 reviews.

price_details: Their site is inquire only, no set pricing published anywhere I could find. They do shoot documentary, photojournalistic and candid style, and offer two different video add ons, a cinematic Rustic cut and a more true to life Timeless documentary cut.
notes: This team's site has no actual pricing, just a check availability button, so we went digging through reviews instead. They shoot hundreds of weddings, elopements and engagements across Colorado every year and offer both photo and video, with two video looks to choose from, a dramatic Rustic cut or a documentary style Timeless cut. One bride said her original photographer flaked three weeks out and this team stepped in with Hunter, photos back in 2 to 3 weeks. A different review praised Angela specifically for staying calm and warm all day.

price_details: No pricing anywhere on their site, just galleries from real Fort Collins shoots. Couldn't find any packages or hourly rates listed.
notes: Patrick leads this Fort Collins team alongside a few other named shooters, Alex, Alec and Becca, and they cover both photo and video on the same wedding. Reviewers keep bringing up candid as the word they wanted, saying they hoped for real emotion over stiff staged smiles and got exactly that. One couple who called themselves camera awkward said the team put them completely at ease. Their galleries show elopement and engagement work at spots like Rist Canyon Inn around Fort Collins.

price_details: No pricing listed on her site at all, you'd have to call or email directly. Only 9 Google reviews total, and most aren't about weddings.
notes: Brenda is based in Telluride but her reviews skew mostly toward senior portraits and general local praise rather than actual wedding days. One review does mention a bride who booked her for a Colorado wedding and then had a serious medical emergency 25 days before the date
photos: though it never says what happened with the wedding itself. Thin on wedding specific feedback either way.

price_details: No pricing posted anywhere on the site. Elopement packages bundle in planning help, local vendor recommendations, and their own Telluride Elopement Guide.
notes: Wren (with Corey showing up alongside her in one review) specializes in adventure elopements around Telluride at named spots like Trout Lake
photos: Bridal Veil Waterfall and Mount Sneffels. Multiple reviewers praised how fast she responds and how quick the turnaround is on the actual photos. One bride specifically said she found Wren through Google and got a reply almost immediately. Another shoot happened during smoky wildfire conditions and the photographer reportedly rolled with it and still delivered.

venue: Preston Benson Videography: Durango Video Production
price_details: Site lists wedding films starting at $3,000 and photography starting at $2,100, everything past those numbers is inquire only. price_details: Nothing about pricing on the site, just galleries and an albums page. Fine art 10x10 wedding albums are offered as a physical add on product.
notes: Kaycee is based in Ridgway but shoots weddings and engagements around Telluride too, and does fine art 10x10 wedding albums as an add on, which isn't that common anymore. One review specifically called out her studio series as a standout, said she's one of the only photographers doing that locally. Reviewers repeatedly mention her creativity and how comfortable she makes people feel, though nobody in the reviews mentions actual pricing or turnaround time.

price_text: Hybrid photo+video add on from $1,100, full packages by inquiry
price_details: Full package pricing isn't posted, she sends a detailed breakdown once you inquire. Hybrid photo and video coverage can be added to any package starting at $1,100. An elopement focused package includes a digital album and high res downloads. Booking requires a signed contract plus a 50% retainer.
notes: Becky (Rebeca Velie) is based in Golden and offers an elopement focused package with a digital album and high res downloads bundled in, alongside full weddings by inquiry. You can add hybrid photo and video coverage to any package starting at $1,100, though the actual full package pricing isn't posted anywhere, she sends a detailed breakdown once you reach out. Booking needs a signed contract plus a 50% retainer. Reviews consistently describe her as calming for couples who are uncomfortable in front of a camera, with one reviewer specifically calling her organized, punctual, and easy to adjust plans with on the fly.

price_text: $1,250-$6,500 depending on hours and add ons
price_details: Half day (4 hours) runs $2,500. Full day (10 hours plus an engagement session) runs $6,500. Standalone engagement sessions run $1,250 for 2 hours or $1,500 for an adventure style shoot. Custom packages and print packages available by inquiry.
notes: Joshua shoots half days at $2,500 for 4 hours up to a full day at $6,500 which bundles in an engagement session, and standalone engagement sessions run $1,250 to $1,500 depending on whether you want the adventure style version. Reviews are unusually consistent about him calming nervous couples down fast, one bride said the photos look editorial but the day itself felt relaxed rather than staged. Another couple booked him for engagement, rehearsal dinner, and wedding photos in one package and said their guests commented on how comfortable he made everyone feel.

price_text: $1,800-$4,500 elopement packages, $650-$3,000 event and family packages
price_details: Elopement tiers: Intimate Story $1,800 for 3 hours, Full Story $3,000 for 5 hours, Epic Story $4,500, all based within 100 miles of Denver. Event and family tiers: Essentials $650 for 1 hour, Main Event $1,500 for 3 hours, Full Experience $3,000. Standalone family, senior and engagement sessions run $550-$1,500. Sessions 50-100 miles from Denver add a stated $100 travel fee, farther than that requires a direct ask. Deposit is either $50-$100 flat or 25%, depending on the package.
notes: Ryan's elopement tiers start at a 3 hour Intimate Story for $1,800 and go up through a 5 hour Full Story at $3,000 and an Epic Story at $4,500, all built around being within 100 miles of Denver. His family and event packages are priced separately, from $650 for an hour up to $3,000 for the full experience session. Reviews call out his communication by name, one couple booked him from California ahead of a Colorado trip and said he had location ideas ready before they even landed. Sessions 50 to 100 miles out add a flat stated $100 travel fee, beyond that you have to ask.

price_details: Site says weddings and elopements are starting at a certain price but the actual number never showed up on the page I checked, so it's inquire only. Second shooter and heirloom album are offered as named add ons, and galleries are delivered 6-8 weeks after the event.
notes: Sam is based in Ouray and leans hard into elopements, her site reads almost like a mission statement about a couple's one real day of memories rather than a sales pitch. She offers an optional second shooter and a heirloom album add on, and delivers galleries in 6 to 8 weeks. One reviewer described a destination micro wedding around 20 guests where Sam sent over a digital planning guide ahead of time so they could visualize locations before flying in. Several reviews mention meeting her first at a friend's wedding and booking her off that alone.

price_details: Site lists weddings at $3,000-$6,000 and elopements at $500 an hour, with engagement sessions bundled into some packages. No separate deposit info was listed on the pricing page.
notes:
-fort collins based, does weddings/families/babies/seniors/elopements/boudoir too
-elopement pricing is hourly ($500/hr) instead of a flat package, worth asking what that covers
-55 reviews all 5 stars, one bride specifically called out the candid guided shots and nonstop energy during the day
-another reviewer got the "silver package" w/ engagement session included and loved it

price_text: Packages start at $3,000, most couples invest ~$6,800
price_details: Colorado wedding packages start at $3,000 with most couples landing around $6,800. Site states no travel fees within Colorado for weddings booked at 6+ hours. Full pricing isn't published, you request a guide to see the tiers.
notes: Based in Berthoud but the site markets statewide, "ALL COLORADO WEDDING PHOTOGRAPHY PACKAGES." One reviewer said Sarah was organized and professional from the first video call, another praised her punctuality and how she stuck to the timeline. A much older review mentioned fast turnaround on both engagement and wedding photos, so that seems consistent over time.

price_details: No flat packages on site, coverage is custom built per couple since a short elopement and a full day wedding are priced differently. The one hard number given is video-only, starting at $4,000 and running $8,000-$9,000 for full-day storytelling. The site does state travel is always included in their proposals.
notes: Breckenridge based but this team works destination weddings, per their own site copy they work with couples all over the world. They offer 2nd/3rd photographers, albums, wall art and welcome dinner coverage as add-ons, plus standalone or bundled video. One review described handling unexpected storms and a timeline shuffle without missing a beat during a Colorado wedding, and another mentioned capturing a whole Vail wedding from the village to the chapel.

price_text: Full day coverage from $4,875, most invest ~$7,500
price_details: Full day coverage starts at $4,875 with most couples spending around $7,500. Every collection includes full day coverage plus roughly 50-75 images per hour with no cap on the total delivered. Site states no travel fees within Colorado and frequent travel to worldwide destinations for engagement sessions.
notes: Fort Collins based but covers Denver and statewide Colorado work too, 122 reviews and still all 5 stars recently. One review said he told the story of the day rather than just shooting it, another couple used him for both engagement and wedding photos and called out his eye for backgrounds specifically.

price_details: Site is entirely elopement-focused messaging, no dollar figures published anywhere. Planning assistance is included with every elopement package per their own copy, but rates require an inquiry.
notes: Montrose based and squarely an elopement and adventure wedding specialist, not a full traditional wedding photographer. Reviews consistently single out photographer Malachi by name and mention his nighttime star photography as a specialty. One review described him as extremely knowledgeable about a Moab elopement location and helping plan the full day, so he clearly travels outside Colorado for the right shoot.

price_details: -site doesn't list actual dollar figures, just "click here for pricing"\n-what they do say: 8hr to full day coverage, destination packages available, film (35mm + medium format) included in ALL packages\n-off season/midweek/micro wedding pricing options exist too

price_details: No pricing published on site at all, just a prompt asking what kind of investment you're looking to make. Couldn't get an actual number to check against.

price_details: No pricing lines were found on their site at all, so there's nothing to go on besides reviews. You'd have to reach out directly for a quote.

Photographer Chris comes up in every review, one mentioned him wading into an icy river to get an engagement shot.

Essential Collection is $2,500 with additional hours at $750 each. Engagement sessions start separately at $500. Site says packages are flexible if you need something different, you just have to contact them.
photos: Colorado Springs based, photographer Jerry comes up by name in every review. 231 reviews and consistently 5 stars across weddings and family sessions both, which says something about volume. One reviewer called him easy going but professional and said he brings infectious energy to a shoot, another said he has an eye for scenery that surpasses others they've used.

price_details: No pricing on site. One review does say his prices are reasonable but gives no actual number to cite, so pricing is on inquiry.
notes: Estes Park based, photographer Alex with reportedly 20+ years experience. Reviews on hand are mostly proposal and engagement shoots around RMNP and Marina Park rather than confirmed full weddings, so it's worth asking directly whether he covers full ceremonies. One reviewer who is a professional photographer herself said the whole planning-to-shoot process went flawlessly for her own proposal and engagement session.

price_details: Site says couples average $5,000-$7,000+ for weddings and $3,000+ for elopements. A la carte options include a $450 engagement session add-on if booked alongside the wedding or elopement. Family and maternity sessions run separately starting at $550 for one hour. Travel can be added to any package, she'll meet you wherever you want.
notes: Denver based photojournalist style shooter for weddings and elopements, her own galleries specifically feature Rocky Mountain National Park and Denver rooftop elopements. Minimum 65 images delivered per hour of coverage. One reviewer flew her out to shoot a wedding in Detroit and said she met with them multiple times beforehand to nail down what they wanted. Another called her transparent on pricing and reliable, and a third said she provided a full elopement guide and helped build the day's timeline.

price_details: Packages scale with hours and group size, with hourly rates available if you want less coverage than the packages offer. A second photographer is recommended once you hit 100+ guests and costs $800 for a full day or $500 for a half day. Elopement packages are priced separately on the site.
notes: Photographer Andy covers Durango plus Silverton, Ouray, Telluride, Ridgway, Hesperus and Mancos, and will also travel into Utah and New Mexico for elopements. One review described a 16 person family shoot for a 50th anniversary where he helped pick a location on short notice, another said he shot a rustic wedding at Eureka Lodge in Silverton and knew the venue and area well enough to find the best angles.

price_details: Their site lists photo packages plus aerial video, aerial photography, and Matterports as add-ons, but says an actual price brochure is coming soon, so nothing is posted yet.
notes: Katie is the photographer behind Studio Kiva and she also offers Matterport and aerial video for anyone who wants drone shots of the venue. All 13 reviews on Google are five stars, several calling out her warmth and how relaxed she makes the process. One reviewer specifically said her pricing felt fair for the quality, but you will have to reach out directly since nothing is posted online yet.

price_details: Elopement coverage starts with an average investment around $5,800. Another review specifically praised how she captures movement and color and how at ease she makes nervous subjects feel.

price_details: Her site has a pricing and info page but it only lists that she helps with elopement planning and guidance, no actual numbers, so you'd need to email her directly for rates.

price_details: Site says to reach out directly to book a session and doesn't list any package pricing for weddings or elopements.

Reviews are mostly about family and anniversary sessions rather than actual weddings, with people praising Chris for working quickly with kids and bringing along drone equipment. Sounds solid for photo plus video coverage but you're inquiring blind on cost since nothing is published.

price_details: Couldn't get their pricing page to load, and no numbers show up anywhere else, so you'd be inquiring without a ballpark going in.
