She covers weddings, engagements, and elopements and says she'll travel anywhere if your wedding is out of Colorado, one review even mentions she shot a wedding out in Charlottesville, VA. Reviews are a mix of wedding and family session feedback, several say she makes people feel at ease and barely noticeable while shooting.,,site homepage copy; google reviews

notes: Three inclusive packages starting at $5,000, with custom quotes available if your coverage doesn't fit the standard hourly tiers. A phone call is offered before booking if you want to talk it through first. She shoots both film and digital and specializes in Scotland and Ireland elopements specifically, though she says she'll travel wherever, and she also thrifts vintage photo albums and handwrites love letters for her couples as a keepsake.
photos: Mandie runs Thistle and Pine out of Colorado Springs and her site leans hard into a nostalgic, film-forward style, one review called it capturing things in an authentic yet elevated way rather than a staged version of the day. A couple from Massachusetts said their communication with her from Colorado was excellent even before they met in person for the actual shoot. Multiple reviews specifically praised her humor and how she made posing feel less intimidating for camera-shy people.

notes: Collections start at $5,850 with your date locked in via signed contract and a retainer. The site notes separate permit fees for certain shoot locations, $50 for one and $300 for another, though it doesn't say which spots those apply to. She's based in Colorado Springs but says she's available worldwide for destination weddings and elopements.
photos: One review said choosing Tina was the single most agonized-over decision of the whole wedding, more than the dress or the venue, and it paid off. Another mentions Summer, who appears to be a second shooter on her team, traveling offsite with the couple for mountain photos and even helping bustle the bride's dress. A different reviewer said Tina handled a wedding-day hiccup professionally, which is a good sign for how she reacts when something actually goes wrong.

price_details: Site quotes wedding photography in the Beaver Creek and Boulder markets typically running $2,500 to $9,000. The specific package listed is 7 hours of coverage for $3,450, with fully edited images delivered within 72 hours, high resolution and print ready. He also has dedicated pricing pages for Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat Springs, and Vail, so this looks like a mountain-town-focused operation with Fort Collins as his base.
notes: Tom covers a wide swath of Colorado ski towns and one review said his images balance traditional posed shots with more candid emotional moments. A standout story: one couple's original photographer's business closed two months before their wedding, and they said Tom stepped in, was warm and responsive from the first call, and put them at ease during what could have been a stressful scramble. Another review mentioned he calls ahead of the wedding to walk through the day's timeline, which is a nice touch if you don't have a planner.
sources: site pricing pages; google reviews; launchintel digest

price_details: checked her site for a rate sheet and there isn't one, just testimonials. you'd have to email her directly for package pricing.
notes: toni shoots in and around vail and she's got a run of 5 star reviews going back years. one couple had her for both their engagement session and a small elopement and said she was easy going and down to earth about the whole thing. another review mentioned she got a quick call back after emailing just two weeks before a proposal, which is a good sign if you're planning something on short notice. seems like a solid pick if you want documentary-feeling coverage without a ton of fuss.

price_text: Wedding packages start around 5 hours coverage; inquire for exact rate
price_details: main wedding packages start at 5 hours and include two photographers (Charlotte plus a second shooter), with smaller custom packages available for less than 5 hours and one shooter if you ask. site doesn't post a dollar figure, just structure: a 1/3 deposit at signing, another 1/3 at the halfway point, final 1/3 due day of, all non-refundable. digital files come full res and unwatermarked, no upsell there. a $400 print credit is included but can't go toward albums.
notes: Charlotte and her team are based in Colorado Springs but a review mentions a destination wedding they shot in Breckenridge where they took charge of scouting locations for an out of state couple. reviews describe her as calm and efficient directing a big group, and one reviewer who'd used her for senior portraits years earlier came back for their wedding. no complaints showed up in what I read, just consistently glowing.

price_text: Wedding collections start at $12,000 for weekday coverage
price_details: that starting price is for a weekday, includes photography from Jesse plus an associate shooter and the first pages of a custom heirloom album already built into the collection. site leans hard into mountain elopement work, framed as a flexible alternative to a full wedding with any location you want.
notes: jesse's reviews go back years and keep circling the same theme, easy to work with and genuinely into the job. one couple hired him for their welcome party the night before and then again the next day for an interactive photo wall, which was apparently a hit with guests. another review from a family session called out his mix of posed and candid shots. nothing but 5 stars in what's up on google.

price_text: Quote only; engagement sessions start at $350
price_details: site doesn't list a wedding number anywhere, engagement sessions are the only figure given at $350 and up. wedding packages come in half day or full day, photo only or photo plus film, and every package is eligible for an 18 month payment plan which is a nice option if you're budgeting early.
notes: Tyler is the photographer behind it and reviews describe him as creative with a strong eye for lighting and angles, styled for editing that leans cinematic. one couple flew him to Hawaii for their wedding after loving their engagement shoot, another had him travel to Montana. seems to be a destination-friendly operation even though he's Colorado Springs based.

price_details: site mentions an hourly starting rate for photo and video but never states the number, you have to go to a separate pricing page to get an actual quote. she describes her style as fly on the wall documentary, capturing the little moments people don't realize are being noticed.
notes: Veronica (goes by V) is based in Grand Junction and covers weddings, elopements, and events, with a second shooter available. one review said she stayed calm and collected through a stressful day and basically ran the whole photography side so the couple could just be present. another mentioned she made a video too, so photo plus video looks to be a standard add rather than a separate service.

price_details: that's the entry point for her Colorado mountain collections, covering elopements, weddings, and family sessions out of Breckenridge, Vail, and Beaver Creek. all collections include some baseline set of deliverables per the site though it doesn't spell out exactly what. booking requires a signed contract and retainer, and she says she responds to inquiries within two business days.
notes: reviews (mostly from family sessions, fewer wedding-specific ones) describe Alex as warm and easy to work with even for out-of-towners who felt out of their element. one reviewer said she rescheduled around bad weather without any hassle. she markets herself specifically toward the laid back, outdoorsy crowd rather than a traditional posed look.

venue: Veils of Vail Wedding Planning & Design

price_details: packages run from $4,700 for 4 hours up to $12,000 for a fully all-inclusive tier with video, hair, makeup, and florals bundled in. the most-booked option is the Wilderness package at $6,000 for 8 hours, which gives room for a hike, a first look, the ceremony, a first dance, and still some breathing room. galleries land in 8-10 weeks, fully edited and unlimited downloads, with handcrafted albums available separately.
notes: Sean runs this solo and focuses entirely on Colorado mountain elopements, with guides on his site for Telluride, Breckenridge, and the San Juans specifically. reviews are extremely consistent, over a hundred of them at 5 stars, and multiple couples mention how fast he responds and how the whole process felt genuine rather than salesy. one review said he helped plan out the actual ceremony flow, not just the photos, which tracks with how elopement-focused photographers often end up doing double duty as day-of planner.

price_text: Base package covers up to 2 hours of coverage
price_details: the base tier is 2 hours with 75+ edited images for the first hour and 50 per additional hour, and there's no travel fee within 2 hours of Denver which covers a lot of the mountain towns. noah, who runs it, says he's been shooting mostly small weddings and elopements the past several years, around 150-200 a year, though he still takes on bigger weddings too.
notes: several reviews come through a specific micro-wedding coordinator (Colorado Micro-Weddings) rather than direct bookings, and describe noah going well past just photos, driving a couple up the mountain for portraits, pinning boutonnieres, even fixing a veil. one out of town couple said he coordinated bringing their flowers to them before the ceremony. sounds like someone who fills in gaps on the day rather than just standing back with a camera.

price_details: packages start as a half day, full day, or multi day concept and then get custom tailored from there, with the full day option being their most popular, described on the site as a full-day adventure anywhere in the US with route mapping built in. they intentionally cap how many elopements they take per year, and shoot in Colorado and Utah as their home base.
notes: run by Andy and Abbi as a team, reviews describe them as warm and low-pressure, no big checklist or forced posing, just working from the key details the couple gives them. one review mentioned a destination shoot in Baja California that spanned two days across multiple locations, another said they traveled for a farm wedding even though that's outside their usual mountain-and-trail aesthetic. consistently called flawless and effortless across the reviews I found.

price_details: nothing on the site about pricing at all, no packages or rate sheet, so you'd need to reach out directly for numbers.
notes: Bex is based in Vail and reviews span family sessions, engagement shoots, and senior portraits going back almost a decade, though I didn't find one specifically describing a full wedding. reviewers consistently mention her flexibility with locations and letting the time of day guide the shoot for better light. one review said the turnaround on a preview gallery was fast enough to be worth noting on its own.

price_details: the site just says inquire for the full price list, no packages posted publicly. booking works through a contract sent electronically plus an online deposit to actually hold your date.
notes: Jessica and Candice run it as a duo out of Fort Collins and reviews describe them nailing engagement shoots, weddings, and even branding headshots for the same couples over time. one review specifically called out how they worked through all the individual and family formals fast, in a short window before losing light, and another mentioned an included engagement session as part of their wedding package. a big pile of 5 star reviews, no complaints turned up.

price_text: 10-hour coverage package with album and engagement session included
price_details: the package includes a complimentary engagement session, a 9x12 custom heirloom album, and no additional travel fees, delivering roughly 60 edited images per hour of coverage which is a useful benchmark for gallery size. Kim and Kyle shoot as a pair out of Fort Collins.
notes: one reviewer, a close friend of theirs, still tried to keep the review professional and said Kim has a strong eye for color and detail. another described the shoot as feeling like an adventure rather than a formal session, and a third called out how good Kim was at picking unique locations. reviews are thin in number (about 10) but everything found was 5 stars.

price_text: Starting at $1,200 per a third-party pricing digest
price_details: site itself doesn't list pricing, so that number comes secondhand rather than off wildirismedia.com directly. Mike runs it solo with an editorial background that shows up in his style, described in reviews as a fly on the wall approach that leans into candid, unplanned moments.
notes: a couple who hired him said he was communicative and laid back and matched whatever they were looking for rather than pushing his own vision. a separate review from a planner who worked alongside him at a Boulder wedding echoed the same, quick to communicate and prioritized the couple's must-have shots. reviews are few (around 7) but all five stars so far.
sources: site homepage; google reviews; launchintel digest

price_details: their site would not load pricing for me so I could not find package numbers, this looks like an inquire-for-a-quote situation for elopement coverage
notes:
-found 2 reviews for ashley & graham, both 5 star, both elopements
-one couple got married at a mountain creek in winter park and said it rained but they still called the photos raw and full of joy
-a second review says graham & ashley helped pick the date/time around crowds AND helped scout a location even with wildfires happening in the area that summer
-no pricing on the site for me to go off of, seems like an inquire situation

price_text: $850 Boulder engagement sessions, elopement packages custom from there
price_details: adventure engagement sessions are 1.5 hrs at $1,200 (or 50% off if bundled with a wedding package), boulder-specific engagement sessions are $850, elopement packages are customizable starting at a 2 hour minimum and include travel within 100 miles of Denver, location planning, and a full online gallery with printing rights. full wedding package pricing itself is not listed, you inquire for that
notes:
-Longmont-based, says she's shot over 550 weddings & elopements in Colorado over the past decade
-travel radius covers Vail, Colorado Springs, RMNP, Fort Collins w/ extra fees past 100 miles of Denver (Crested Butte, Telluride, Durango need a special inquiry)
-site copy leans hard into "unscripted" and documenting real experiences over a produced day
-elopement packages bundle in an online gallery w/ printing rights, which is a nice touch

price_text: Minimum investment $2,700 for micro wedding/elopement, full wedding days start at $2,600
price_details: elopement/micro wedding planning starts at a $2,700 minimum investment, full wedding day coverage starts at $2,600 and scales up from a consult. 25% non-refundable deposit at booking, balance due one month out. a complimentary engagement session is bundled into most wedding packages. no travel fee anywhere in the contiguous US, domestic travel outside Colorado runs $800, international is $2,000
notes:
-based out of Aspen/Vail, leans into a whole micro-wedding + elopement planning angle, not just photography
-says she has a network of other vendors across the country and internationally for destination stuff
-good detail: no travel fee within the contiguous US at all, which is unusual, but it jumps to $800 once you leave Colorado and $2k international
-nothing from actual couples yet, this is all from her own site copy

price_details: her site itself just says to contact her for pricing, no packages listed there. a pricing digest I found puts her starting range at $1,500-$2,300 for weddings and elopements, but that's not confirmed straight from her
notes:
- Denver-based, studio address is listed as 3282 W Oxford Ave
- fine art style is the label she uses for herself
- also shoots pet, family, lifestyle, branding, product and landscape work, so weddings aren't her only thing
- explicitly women-owned and LGBTQ+ friendly on her materials
- no reviews turned up in what I checked, so can't vouch for the actual experience yet

price_details: her 6-hour package is pitched for smaller/intimate weddings and elopements, and a second shooter option adds 8 hours of coverage. a pricing digest puts starting packages around $1,800-$2,500 including 2 photographers, an engagement session, print release and consultation, with a 4-6 week turnaround, though I didn't get that confirmed directly off her site's numbers
notes:
-documentary/photojournalistic style, shooting weddings and video together since 2018

-booking flow is a consult first, then she sends a proposal, you sign + pay retainer to lock the date
-open to fully custom packages if the standard ones don't fit, which is a nice flexible touch

price_details: a pricing digest lists her starting rate at $3,200 for wedding and elopement work, though her site itself doesn't spell out exact package numbers beyond travel add-ons. travel outside her included radius runs $50 per hour of driving time when it's not bundled into the package already
notes:
-based in Windsor / Northern Colorado, says she's shot 180+ weddings and elopements
-style is warm and colorful, her words, going for fun and love over anything moody
-she'll travel basically anywhere, and Colorado + Washington "bucket list" locations get 25% off the listed package
-24-hour gallery turnaround is her headline stat, at least for proposal sessions, which is fast

price_details: a pricing digest puts her starting rate at $2,900, her own site doesn't list exact numbers, just says packages get built around a consult once hours and photographer count are locked in. deposit is 30% of the total, due at booking
notes:
-based in Aspen, serves Glenwood Springs and Carbondale too, photojournalistic is the style word she uses
-14 years of experience across elopement, event, journalism and fashion photography, she got her photography degree from Colorado Mountain College in 2015
-engagement sessions can be tacked on for an extra fee if not already bundled
-no couple reviews turned up in what I found, so take the experience claims at face value for now

price_details: a WPJA-sourced digest puts her starting rate at $6,400. her site itself doesn't list numbers, but breaks down what's included: doc-style collections go up to 12 hours of coverage plus an engagement session and handcrafted layflat albums, while cheaper "associate" collections cap at 10 hours and hand the shoot to one of her associates instead of Autumn or Griffin themselves
notes:
-both are former photojournalists and lean hard into documentary style, real moments over posed ones
-they send a hard copy planning guide when you book, with a planning spreadsheet, vow-writing tips and engagement session location ideas, kind of a nice extra
-associate collections exist specifically for when they're already booked or your budget is lower, worth asking which tier you're actually getting
-no couple reviews came up in what I checked, just the site's own package copy

price_details: front range weddings start at $6k. photo-only collections run $6.8k-9.3k, and bundling photo & video runs $11k-15.5k. all collections include full day coverage, listed as 9-12 hours, and some collections add on extras beyond that base
notes: -a review quoted on her own site says she went above and beyond and "felt like a welcome friend" on the wedding day itself, so take that with a grain of salt since it's her own testimonial, not independently sourced\n-she's upfront that she only shot elopements for a few years after her first wedding experience, her words were it made her do a "dramatic 180"\n-front range is the stated base area, pricing is on the higher end compared to what else I've seen in this range

price_text: $2,000-$4,250 for named packages, elopement from ~$300 (film) or bundled into the 3-hour tier
price_details: packages are literally named after mountains: Dinosaur Ridge is $2,000, South Table Mountain is $3,250, Lookout Mountain is $4,250. a 3-hour elopement package exists separately. extra hour of coverage with both Kevin and Michelle (or a second shooter) is $550, engagement/family/senior portraits are $800, a 35mm film add-on starts at $300, albums start at $750, and a wood USB drive is $50. 40% retainer to book, travel is included within 1 hour of Golden and within 2 hours for weddings specifically
notes:
-Golden-based husband/wife team, Kevin Bergthold and Michelle Blomberg, site says 20 years experience and 300+ weddings shot
-listed as a preferred photographer at Boettcher Mansion, Mt Vernon Canyon Club, Evergreen Lake House and the Golden Hotel, so if you're booking one of those venues they'll already know the space
-complimentary engagement session comes with the standard packages
-no couple reviews turned up in what I checked, this is all site pricing copy

price_text: $2,000 retainer to book, full pricing is a custom quote
price_details: no package tiers listed on the site, pricing works as a custom quote after a consultation. what IS published: a $2,000 retainer holds your date, the remaining balance is due two weeks before the wedding, and flexible payment installments are offered
notes:
-works Aspen and the Vail Valley, alpine elopements up through big multi-day resort weekends at spots like Beaver Creek and Bachelor Gulch
-style described as documentary storytelling blended with cinematic, editorial restraint
-elopements get more hands-on treatment, site specifically calls out location scouting and light timing as part of that
-no reviews came up in what I found, so this is all from the site's own pricing/process page

price_details: Wedding packages start at $5,200, elopement packages from $2,400, both custom built per couple. $1,000 retainer holds your date. Add-ons: extra hours $800 (day-of add $1,000), second shooter $200/hr, film rolls $200 each, polaroid packs $50 each. Payment plan available between retainer and one week out.
notes: -based in Mancos/Durango, southwest Colorado, does destination too apparently. -5 years in, used to teach spanish before this lol. -leans into backcountry/off the beaten path locations per WPJA writeup, comes with her own SWCO location guide and an LGBTQ+ affirming vendor list which is a nice touch. -one review calls her elopement work outstanding, ~$3k starting per WPJA which lines up with her site's low end.

Collections begin at $6,500 (site also lists a $6,000 starting figure elsewhere, may be an older number). Every package includes rehearsal coverage. Couldn't find a breakdown of hours or add-ons like second shooter or albums, site keeps it vague and points you to a consult to pick a collection.
photos: Went looking for their editorial stuff since that's what they're known for and the site backs it up, called high-fashion editorial in one summary. Rehearsal day coverage baked into every package is a nice touch most photographers charge extra for. Colorado Springs based. Didn't find a single review, good or bad, just the pricing page and portfolio.

price_details: No dollar figures published, site says packages are never one size fits all so you inquire. Ultimate elopement package is described though: 12 hours of coverage, splittable across two days or a midday break, complimentary album design included.
notes: Kelly is based in Grand Junction and works all over Colorado plus Moab, Utah, so if you want mountain or desert backdrops she's covered. She's not just showing up to shoot, her whole pitch is she helps plan the elopement logistics too (locations, timeline, even the legal paperwork) and she's done 120+ of them. She eloped herself 7 years ago which is clearly where the niche came from. Nature-inspired storytelling is the style word she uses.

price_details: Wedding packages start at $2,800 and include an engagement session, day-of coverage, online gallery and printing rights. Second photographer and/or wedding album are add-ons on some tiers. 25% deposit to book, rest flexible as long as it's paid by the wedding day.
notes: He only takes a limited number of weddings a year by choice, per his own FAQ, so he's not trying to be everywhere. Every package comes with an engagement session and he seems genuinely into those, not just an upsell, his FAQ talks about it being a chance to get comfortable in front of the camera before the big day. Didn't find third party reviews, just his own site copy.

price_details: Full day packages run $4,500 to $7,200 with a minimum of 8 hours coverage, top tier gets you up to 9 hours plus a one hour engagement or adventure session. Lower tiers step down to 6 and 4 hour coverage. Standalone engagement session is $950 for 1.5 hours, 75+ edited photos delivered in 2 weeks. Travel is not included past an hour and a half from Denver, they'll go anywhere but it costs extra past that radius.
notes: Denver based husband and wife team, they specialize in elopements, micro weddings and adventure sessions across the Rockies and say they'll travel worldwide for the right wedding. One digest mentioned sneak peeks come back within days and full galleries are quick too, plus they often shoot with two photographers on full wedding packages. Site is unusually clear about what's included in every tier, no mystery pricing here.

price_details: Peak season (May-Oct) rates by day: Friday $7,500, Saturday $9,000, Sunday $6,500, Mon-Thurs $5,500. Off season drops to Friday/Saturday $5,000, Sunday $4,250, Mon-Thurs $4,000. Partial-day and half to full day rental style options exist too. Elopements are cheaper, one digest put a typical elopement rate under $3k, and a WPJA listing shows a $1,800 starting price which is probably an older or bare-bones figure.
notes: Her whole pricing page is set up as a build your own package, with a breakdown of what a second photographer covers (only for the important parts of the day, timing overlaps with hers). One digest specifically praised how efficient her elopement shoots are, lots of images out of just two hours. Also does a Boulder elopement guide and a Denver elopement/microwedding guide on her site if you're still narrowing down where to go.

price_details: Packages range $5,500 to $12k, site says the average investment for an 8 hour wedding is $8k. 6 hours to full day coverage options. Videography runs $4,000-$8,500 separately or bundled with photo, average listed at $5,500 for half to full day. Retainer is variable by package for weddings, with a payment plan available. Site says travel fees are complimentary.
notes: She shoots digital, film, and Super 8 for weddings and elopements, based in the mountain west but says she travels for destination weddings regularly. Also does boudoir and portraits on the side so weddings aren't her only category, just one of several. Didn't find any actual reviews, just her own site copy laying out the packages.

price_details: Couldn't pin down real Colorado numbers, one digest cites a $3,000 full day rate with no call needed for pricing, but their own site copy is entirely written for Bay Area and San Francisco clients (half day up to 5hrs, full day up to 12hr, 30 day turnaround guarantee). Worth double checking directly whether they still shoot Colorado weddings before you get attached.
notes: Their site reads like it's built for a California client base, not Colorado, so it's a little confusing for a Colorado search result. If the $3,000 full day figure is accurate that's on the affordable end, and packages apparently come with a private online album. Would want to confirm with them directly that they actually travel here before booking.

price_details: Packages start at $4,800 per his own site. One outside pricing summary lists him starting around $5,000, close enough it's probably the same figure rounded. Proposal and contract process is straightforward, you sign online and pay the retainer to lock the date.
notes: Documentary and candid, hands off style is how he's described, and he serves all of Colorado, Colorado Springs based. He's apparently a WPJA top international photographer, which is a real award not just a marketing line. One digest said he's almost fully booked for 2026 already so if that's your year you'd want to move fast.

price_details: In-state Colorado (and Arkansas) elopements start at $750. Out of state elopements scale by hours: $2,250 for 2 hours, $4,200 for 4 hours, $4,000 for 5 hours, up to $5,000 for 5 hours on the higher tier (site lists a couple of overlapping 5 hour figures, worth clarifying which applies). 30% deposit due at signing, has to be paid within 48 hours or the date opens back up.
notes: She shoots both digital and 35mm film and calls her style honest and heartfelt rather than anything trendy. Two testimonials on her site use words like priceless and best investment, which is obviously her own selected quotes so take it as marketing, but the price tiers are laid out clearly which is more than most give you.

price_details: Average investment is $7,750 per her site, no published tier breakdown beyond that headline number. 40% due upfront to book, remainder on a flexible payment plan. You get a meeting link and packages within 48 hours of reaching out.
notes: Denver based, alternative documentary style, and her whole thing is elopements and courthouse weddings specifically, she's built out a full courthouse guide on her site. She also keeps an allied vendor list (musicians, picnic caterers) for pre or post elopement stuff which is a nice extra if you're doing a small day. Didn't find any third party reviews, just her own copy.

price_details: No dollar figures on the pricing page itself, just a philosophy of transparency and a promise everything's customizable by hours and coverage type. What is spelled out: travel within 100 miles of Denver is included in every package, winter mountain trips may add a fee, and no drone coverage is offered at all right now.
notes: She does both digital and film, plus a limited weekday-only family session schedule on the side. Booking is simple, you email or fill out a form, she sends a contract and invoice, and you're booked once you sign and pay the initial deposit. All packages include phone or email consults, timeline help and vendor recommendations, so there's some planning support built in even without a published price.

price_details: couldn't get their pricing page to load and no packages are posted anywhere else i found, so this is inquire-only for now
notes: - Vibe check only: one listing calls the style 'vibrant, true-to-life,' which lines up w/ the portfolio thumbnails - No packages posted anywhere, you'd have to reach out direct for a quote - Colorado Springs based, no hours/coverage specifics available yet

price_details: no packages listed on the actual site, but a WPJA writeup pegs him starting around $4,000. site copy only mentions engagement/couples sessions, no hourly breakdown given
notes: ok so matt's website itself barely has any pricing info on it lol, just some copy about engagement sessions (bring your pets if you want, cute detail). had to go find a WPJA blurb to get an actual number, which said he starts around 4k and has shot in 9 states so he clearly travels a lot. background is apparently in fashion/product photography and he leans on off camera lighting for golden hour type shots, which tracks w/ his site vibe. no reviews turned up so can't speak to how he actually is day of

price_text: Starting around $5,500 per WPJA for micro weddings
price_details: WPJA lists her starting around $5,500. Site is built around Colorado Micro Wedding Collections (30-page and 20-page heirloom album tiers), with albums, engagement add-ons, and rehearsal coverage bolted on separately rather than bundled by default. She sends a full pricing guide only after an initial contact, then does a video call before booking, so there's a screening step before you see real numbers.
notes: Spent a while on Nina's site because she has a whole elopement and micro-wedding angle that's different from most photographers we looked at. She's explicitly LGBTQ+ inclusive with a dedicated page on LGBTQ+ elopements, shoots a film and digital hybrid, and has apparently done 300+ weddings across the Rocky Mountains per WPJA. Turnaround is fast, sneak peeks within two days and the full gallery within six weeks, which is on the quicker end of what we've seen. One nice detail: no travel fee if your elopement is in Aspen. You do have to go through her contact form and a video call before getting real pricing though, it's not just posted up front.

venue: Photography: Family, Engagement, Wedding, Portrait etc.
price_details: no site to check pricing on at all (google listing only), and none of the three reviews we read mention a number. 15 reviews total at 5 stars though, which is a strong signal even without pricing
notes: - Reviewer eloped in Truckee w/ 18 family/friends and said Marta made picking a package easy, asked good questions to get her creative ideas flowing - Another bride said the photos captured the day's vibrancy 'absolutely perfectly' and specifically leaned on Marta for day-of timing advice since they skipped a planner - A separate reviewer hired her for a surprise Father's Day family shoot, said she was great wrangling kids and turned around digital photos within a few days - No site, no pricing anywhere, New Castle based per the listing

price_details: site has actual tiered pricing up front: 4-hour coverage $2,800, 6-hour $3,400, 8-hour $5,200 (the 8-hour is the full-day option, 700+ edited images, online gallery, delivery in 3 weeks). Elopement half-day (up to 4 hrs) is $2,200 and includes a location scouting consult, full day elopement is $3,800. There's also a small wedding/elopement mini session at $1,375 and a 35mm film add-on where pricing depends on film stock cost, inquire direct. Travel fees are case by case depending on where the wedding falls in the year, no flat number given. Non-refundable retainer required to hold the date, standard industry stuff.
notes: Elliot (goes by Henry per the site, a little confusing) documentary-style, described as bold and colorful, been shooting weddings about 10 years. What stood out is the site actually publishes real numbers instead of making you ask, which was rare in our search. He also does custom packages for couples who don't fit a normal mold and specifically says he won't price gouge on travel, for whatever that's worth coming from the vendor themselves. Album design is optional, he'll just point you to a printer if you want to DIY it. No third-party reviews came up in our search so this is mostly straight from his own site.

price_details: - Site has a rates page but it's just a contact form, no actual numbers listed anywhere - All packages apparently include full print/usage rights per the site copy, but you have to fill out the form to get a dollar figure - No reviews found to cross check pricing against

price_details: Site says wedding collections start at $3,800, with custom collections built for couples whose day doesn't fit a set tier, and payment plans available. No hourly breakdown or add-on list given beyond that starting figure.
notes: Went down her site a bit and the style leans lighter and true to color, not overly warm or moody. Wedding collections start at $3,800 but she also builds custom ones and offers payment plans, which is a nice option if the full amount up front is a stretch. A lot of her portfolio examples are engagement sessions (saw one at Brainard Lake specifically), so if you want a preview of how she shoots before booking the wedding day, there's plenty to look at. No reviews came up in what we checked, so take the site's own framing with a grain of salt.

price_text: Elopements $3,000-$10,500 by tier, single Wedding Collection price not listed on site
price_details: Elopement tiers are spelled out: Mini Local $3,000, Half Day $4,200, Full Day $7,000, Multi-Day $10,500. For actual weddings she only offers one Wedding Collection (no hourly tiers to compare), and the site doesn't publish that number, you'd need to inquire. Add-ons are flat $1,200 each regardless of type, covers an engagement session, a post-wedding hiking session, rehearsal dinner coverage, or an album. $1,300 retainer to book, balance due anytime up to 30 days before the wedding.
notes: This one's interesting because she deliberately does ONE wedding collection instead of hourly tiers, her whole pitch is that hourly limits suck and she doesn't want you rushing your timeline to squeeze in photos. Elopement pricing is fully published though and ranges $3,000 to $10,500 depending on mini/half/full/multi-day. She's Colorado focused with a specific mountain elopement angle, her site literally points out that different parts of the state have different rules around elopement permits, which is a detail I hadn't thought about. Add-ons (engagement session, hiking session, rehearsal dinner, album) are all a flat $1,200 each, can be tacked on anytime, not just at booking.

price_text: Weddings start at $3,750, elopements/micro-weddings by inquiry
price_details: Regular weddings start at $3,750 (coverage length not specified beyond 'starting'), micro-weddings under 35 people are quote-only via the photographer (Piper). Engagement sessions start at $400 (1.5 hrs), portrait sessions start at $375 (30 min). Film add-ons run $50-$125 depending on how many rolls of 35mm you want processed and scanned, more available on request. Travel fees are shoot-location dependent, no flat rate given. Rebooking fee is $500 if you reschedule a June-August weekend wedding; deposits are non-refundable on cancellation for both weddings and portraits.
notes: Her editorial style comes through even in the pricing page copy, which reads more personal than most (she's been to 50 countries and clearly loves travel shoots). Weddings start at $3,750 and portraits/engagements are priced separately and lower, $400 and $375 respectively. The film add-on menu is a nice touch if you want some 35mm mixed into a digital package, $50 for one roll up to $125 for three. One wart: if you need to reschedule a summer weekend wedding there's a $500 rebooking fee, and deposits are non-refundable across the board on cancellation, so read the contract terms closely before signing.

price_text: $2,500-$9,000 range depending on resort area, 7-hour package $3,450
price_details: Site breaks pricing out by mountain town (Beaver Creek, Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat, Vail) with the same general range quoted for each: $2,500-$9,000. The one hard package number given is a 7-hour wedding photography package at $3,450, which includes fully edited images delivered within 72 hours, ready to print. No separate line items for engagement sessions or second shooter found.
notes: Tom shoots documentary style and specifically markets himself around the mountain resort towns, Vail, Beaver Creek, Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat all get their own pricing page but the range quoted is the same $2,500 to $9,000 across the board. The one concrete package is 7 hours for $3,450 with a genuinely fast turnaround, 72 hours for fully edited images, which is quicker than most photographers we've looked at. A Fort Collins bride's review mentioned he was reasonably priced, got to know the couple a bit before the wedding, and complimented his angles, so that tracks with the site's own positioning.
service_region: Mountain resort towns (Vail, Beaver Creek, Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat) RICH:tom-k-photo

price_details: Bronze (6 hrs) $2,600, Silver (8 hrs) $3,000, Gold (10 hrs) $3,400, Diamond (10 hrs lead + 5 hrs second photographer) $5,000. Second photographer can also be added a la carte at $150/hr to any tier. 1.5-hour engagement session is a separate $600 add-on. A regional digest lists starting rates as low as $2,100-$2,500 ($2,500 specifically for the Boulder area), so the published site tiers above may reflect a later price update.
notes: Their pricing page is genuinely one of the more transparent ones we found, full tiered breakdown from Bronze up through Diamond with hours and second-shooter inclusion spelled out for each. Style is photojournalistic with warm, true-to-life editing per their own framing, and a digest we found says they've shot 500+ weddings and deliver around 1,300 final edited images with a private gallery (50 sneak peeks within 3 weeks of the wedding). Based in Denver/Broomfield. Only wart is the digest quotes a lower starting price than what's on the live pricing page now, so treat the $2,600 Bronze tier as the more current number and double check before assuming the cheaper figure still applies.

price_details: they've got a price check tool on the site so you can build out the total yourself. engagement session add-on is $225, flash drive is $60, videography add-on is $1,900. 50% deposit due at booking. travel fee kicks in for anything outside denver metro, min $100, plus a $150 lodging fee for early/late calls and a $200 long day fee if the event runs over
notes: -multi-photographer brand, not a single shooter - they've got people in arvada, colorado springs & windsor so you may not know who you get til closer to the date -price check tool on the site is nice, lets you build your own package before calling anyone -knot best of weddings hall of fame per their listing, for what that's worth -videography got added as an add-on in 2025 if you want both

price_details: Six hours of coverage is $3,000, eight hours is $3,500, ten hours is $4,000, and the ten hour package includes a complimentary one hour local engagement session. You can add an extra hour of shooting for $300, a second shooter for $1,000, and family photos are a $400 add on if you need them separately. Photo rights come with every package, which was a nice surprise since some photographers charge extra for that.
notes: Found a mention that she shot a wedding at Sapphire Point, which is a gorgeous spot near Breckenridge, and the review called her well priced for the area. The tiered hourly structure makes it easy to compare against other quotes since everything is spelled out on her site instead of a call required situation. No specific style word turned up in what I found, just that people are happy with the value.
